entry.title: State Cyber Leaders Beg Congress for More Funding, Support
entry.content:
A recent congressional hearing highlighted how states are reeling from federal cutbacks to important cyber grants and information sharing initiatives amid damaging attacks to critical infrastructure.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Feeding Frenzy: 'Megalodon' Malware Infects Thousands of GitHub Repos
entry.content:
In just six hours, the campaign quietly pushed thousands of malicious commits to more than 5,500 GitHub repositories, stealing credentials, developer secrets, and more.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cybersecurity Evolution: How We Went From Perimeter Defense to AI-Native Security
entry.content:
The cybersecurity industry of 2006 barely resembled today's billion-dollar behemoth. As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary celebration, we trace the industry's evolution through a technology lens.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: AI-Assisted Exploit Development Outpaces Scanner Detection
entry.content:
Attackers are using AI to dramatically reduce the time they need to develop a working exploit for a CVE, according to new research.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Latin American Cybercriminals Hoover Up Government Data
entry.content:
A purported leak exposing 5.8 million records of Uruguayan citizens is the latest incident where cybercriminals targeted government agencies to monetize citizen data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ransomware Actors Show Up In Person to Steal Law Firm Data
entry.content:
The FBI warned that the extortion gang Silent Ransom Group is targeting law firms and socially engineering its way into servers and databases.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Nordic CISOs Handle Rising Cyber Threats Remarkably Well
entry.content:
Artificial intelligence notwithstanding, the vast majority of CISOs in northern Europe say they're facing no more serious cyberattacks than they did two years ago.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘SymJack’ Attack Turns AI Coding Agents Into Supply Chain Attack Delivery Systems
entry.content:
Malicious repositories and disguised symlinks can trick AI coding agents into silently installing attacker-controlled MCP servers capable of stealing secrets, compromising CI pipelines, and deploying malicious code. The post ‘SymJack’ Attack Turns AI Coding Agents Into Supply Chain Attack Delivery Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: The Credential Crisis: How Stolen Credentials Defeat Modern Security
entry.content:
As AI accelerates phishing, session hijacking, and credential abuse, security teams are racing to close the gap between attacker speed and defensive response. The post The Credential Crisis: How Stolen Credentials Defeat Modern Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Romanian Hacker Sentenced to Prison in US for Selling Access to State Network
entry.content:
Catalin Dragomir previously pleaded guilty to selling access to an Oregon state government office’s network. The post Romanian Hacker Sentenced to Prison in US for Selling Access to State Network appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: SecurityWeek to Host AI Risk Summit August 11-12 at the Ritz-Carlton, Half Moon Bay
entry.content:
Now in its third year, the AI Risk Summit is the leading conference that brings together CISOs, security leaders, AI researchers, developers, policymakers, and enterprise risk professionals. The post SecurityWeek to Host AI Risk Summit August 11-12 at the Ritz-Carlton, Half Moon Bay appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Vulnerability in Popular Conference Software Granted Attackers a 100% Talk Acceptance Rate
entry.content:
Novee researchers discovered an account takeover vulnerability in the open source CFP management tool Pretalx. The post Vulnerability in Popular Conference Software Granted Attackers a 100% Talk Acceptance Rate appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: UK Cyberspying Chief Calls AI ‘an Unstoppable Force’ and Warns About Russia
entry.content:
The speech is the latest in a string of warnings from intelligence experts that Russia is stepping up hostile activity in a “gray zone” that falls just below the threshold of war. The post UK Cyberspying Chief Calls AI ‘an Unstoppable Force’ and Warns About Russia appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Google Unveils AI Threat Defense Platform to Fight AI-Powered Cyberattacks
entry.content:
New AI Threat Defense platform combines capabilities from Mandiant, Wiz and Gemini to help customers fight AI with AI. The post Google Unveils AI Threat Defense Platform to Fight AI-Powered Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iranian intelligence service behind hack of LA transit system, researchers say
entry.content:
The hacking group claimed to be a standalone hacktivist crew but actually has ties to the Ministry of Intelligence of the Islamic Republic of Iran (MOIS), researchers at Gambit Security said in a report published Tuesday.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Dutch police arrest man over cyber breach at Ajax football club
entry.content:
The suspect was detained in the central Dutch town of Buren, where law enforcement officers also searched his home and seized multiple digital storage devices, according to a statement released Tuesday by the Dutch National Police.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: FBI warns extortion hackers are visiting US law firms to steal data
entry.content:
In a public advisory issued Tuesday the FBI said a hacking group has targeted law firms using social engineering schemes to gain remote access to corporate systems and exfiltrate data.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Rudd orders Cyber Command reviews as Pentagon presses reform agenda
entry.content:
Army Gen. Joshua Rudd, who took the twin-leadership reins of Cyber Command and the NSA in March, recently tapped MITRE to conduct a potentially wide-ranging review into the organization, according to three people familiar with the matter.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Romanian national sentenced to more than 4 years for hacking Oregon government systems
entry.content:
Dragomir was arrested in Romania in November 2024 and brought to the U.S. last year to face charges for hacking into the network belonging to Oregon’s Office of Emergency Management.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
entry.content:
Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
entry.content:
A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
entry.content:
An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
entry.content:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
entry.content:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
entry.content:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Radiology Associates of Richmond Data Breach Affects 266K Individuals
entry.content:
Radiology Associates of Richmond in Virginia, one of the oldest, continuously operating private radiology practices in the United States, has […] The post Radiology Associates of Richmond Data Breach Affects 266K Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: May 2026 Data Breach Round Up: Data Breaches Affect 9 HIPAA-regulated Entities
entry.content:
A round-up of data breaches recently announced by 9 HIPAA-regulated entities: University of Nebraska Medical Center, Singing River Health System, […] The post May 2026 Data Breach Round Up: Data Breaches Affect 9 HIPAA-regulated Entities appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Datavant Group to Pay $900,000 to Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
A settlement has been agreed to resolve a class action lawsuit against Ciox Health, which does business as Datavant Group, […] The post Datavant Group to Pay $900,000 to Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: HIPAA Certification for Business Associates
entry.content:
HIPAA certification for Business Associates is documented evidence that employees have completed training on HIPAA Privacy Rule, HIPAA Security Rule, […] The post HIPAA Certification for Business Associates appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Mission Community Hospital Pays $1.55M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
Deanco Healthcare, LLC, the operator of Mission Community Hospital, an acute care hospital serving patients in the San Fernando Valley […] The post Mission Community Hospital Pays $1.55M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: OCR Reports to Congress on HIPAA Compliance and Data Breaches in 2024
entry.content:
The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) has submitted its annual reports to Congress […] The post OCR Reports to Congress on HIPAA Compliance and Data Breaches in 2024 appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: The Oncology Institute Confirms Unauthorized Access to Systems Due to Vendor Breach
entry.content:
The Oncology Institute, a publicly traded provider of cancer care through more than 100 clinics in California, Oregon, Nevada, Arizona, […] The post The Oncology Institute Confirms Unauthorized Access to Systems Due to Vendor Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Connecticut Medicaid Portal Breach Affects 22,500 Hartford HealthCare Patients
entry.content:
The personal and protected health information of approximately 22,500 Hartford HealthCare patients has been exposed in a security incident. Data […] The post Connecticut Medicaid Portal Breach Affects 22,500 Hartford HealthCare Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Microsoft Defender can now automatically isolate hacked endpoints
entry.content:
Microsoft is testing a new Defender for Endpoint capability that will automatically isolate compromised endpoints to thwart attackers' attempts to move laterally across the network. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Charter confirms data breach after ShinyHunters extortion threat
entry.content:
U.S. telecommunications giant Charter Communications has confirmed it suffered a data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion group threatened to leak stolen data unless a ransom is paid. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Dutch police arrests suspect linked to Ajax football club hack
entry.content:
The Dutch National Police arrested a 35-year-old man suspected of hacking the professional football club Ajax Amsterdam (AFC Ajax) earlier this year. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: FBI warns of in-person data theft attacks from extortion gang
entry.content:
The FBI warned on Tuesday that the Silent Ransom Group (SRG) extortion gang is now targeting U.S.-based law firms in in-person data theft attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: UK: Victims feel ‘violated’ after water firm’s data breach
entry.content:
Oprah Flash reports: “Violated” and being “unable to trust” have been the feelings plaguing victims of a cyber attack on a Midlands-based water company. The personal data of 633,887 people was stolen and published on the dark web, after South Staffs Water was hacked in 2020. Customers said they faced a deluge of scam emails... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Rhode Island’s workers’ compensation notifies those affected by January data breach
entry.content:
Rhode Island residents may understandably wonder about the state’s vendor security monitoring. First, it was the Deloitte and the RIBridges data breach that affected more than 730,000 residents. Now the vendor that administers the state’s workers’ compensation insurance has disclosed a breach affecting 131,000 residents, including 4,500 former and current state employees. Alexander Castro reports:... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: UK: £355,880.10 confiscation order secured following proceeds of crime hearing
entry.content:
There’s a follow-up to the case of a motor insurance worker who received a suspended prison sentence for unlawfully accessing personal information. On May 21, the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) announced that it had secured a £355,880.10 confiscation order against the former Manchester motor insurance worker, Rizwan Manjra. A statement by the ICO indicates that... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: France Sees More Violent Attacks on Crypto Holders Than Any Other Country
entry.content:
Julian Lim reports: The hardest part of crypto security used to be keeping private keys away from hackers. In France, the problem has moved much closer to home. A new report says roughly 70% of documented wrench attacks against crypto holders and their families are happening there, turning a niche security term into a very... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: PowerSchool’s $17.25 Million Settlement Exposes Years of Student Data Tracking
entry.content:
If you ask most people what breach PowerSchool experienced, their first response might be the 2024 hacking incident that affected tens of millions of students. But even before that breach, there was another significant breach involving PowerSchool that began in 2021. Colin Lee and Koji Edmunds report: In early April, many students across the world... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Lithuania investigates theft of 600,000 state registry records
entry.content:
Daryna Antoniuk reports: The Lithuanian Prosecutor General’s Office said Friday that attackers gained unauthorized access to more than 600,000 records managed by the Centre of Registers, the state agency responsible for handling property and legal entity records. Prosecutors said the breach involved the misuse of login credentials assigned to institutions authorized to access the databases, and likely... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Malware seller known as “Venom” extradited to France
entry.content:
There is an update to an arrest made in Greece in November as part of Operation Endgame. Ekathimerini reports: A 39-year-old Albanian national known online as “Venom” was extradited to France in mid-May after his arrest last November at his apartment in the Nikaia district of Athens. The suspect, who described himself as a construction... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: UK Visa Portal spilled thousands of applicants’ passports and selfies online — and hasn’t fixed the leak
entry.content:
Zack Whittaker reports: A website called UK Visa Portal is publicly exposing the passports and selfie photos of applicants who signed up and paid the site to obtain a U.K immigration visa, TechCrunch has learned. An anonymous person notified TechCrunch about the security lapse, saying that the website is exposing at least 100,000 documents from... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Silent Ransom Group Impersonating IT Personnel through Social Engineering
entry.content:
The FBI has issued a Flash Alert about the Silent Ransom Group. Summary The Silent Ransom Group (SRG), also known as Luna Moth, Chatty Spider, and UNC3753, is targeting law firms using social engineering techniques. Through phone calls and phishing emails, SRG actors pose as IT support to establish access to victim computers and exfiltrate... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: NL: Schiphol cargo worker arrested over alleged data leaks to drug networks
entry.content:
NL Times reports: The Royal Netherlands Marechaussee detained a 24-year-old Amsterdam-based cargo worker at Schiphol on Tuesday, May 19, on suspicion of unauthorized access to computer systems and the leaking of confidential company information, Luchtvaart Nieuws has reported. According to the ongoing investigation, the suspect allegedly used his access to a cargo handling company’s systems at the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Funke Scheid Report Victim funke-scheid.de Threat Actor Bavacai Date Discovered May 26, 2026 Description Funke Scheid is an industrial manufacturing company based in Germany, â¦May 26, 2026
entry.content:

entry.content:

📍 Location: California | 📰 Source: California AG | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach
